enterprise.name: Pasture Beef Enterprise
enterprise.description: A grazing beef enterprise: enteric fermentation and manure emissions from the herd, plus the farm's energy, waste and upstream (Scope 3) emissions.

customSheets.name: Results
customSheets.description: The single enterprise-wide results tab, unique to this enterprise. Module Results tabs are NOT imported; this sheet reads each module's result cells directly by named range.

customSheets.name: Input - Enterprise
customSheets.description: Optional enterprise-level input sheet for values shared across modules. Imported module input sheets are kept as-is alongside this one.

modules.comment: Enteric and Manure both ship an 'Input - Pasture Beef' sheet that backs the same 17 named ranges, but 7 of them sit at different rows (Enteric's date/digestibility block is ~7 rows higher than Manure's). Manure is the master: it keeps the canonical 'Input - Pasture Beef' sheet and owns those named ranges. Enteric's copy is imported under a distinct name via renameSheets so it does not collide; the builder drops the renamed copy's duplicate name definitions (master keeps them) and repoints Enteric's own formulas to this copy. Remove this rename once a single enterprise-level input sheet replaces the per-module input sheets.

Pasture beef only needs the pasture residue chain. The workbook now ships crop- and pasture-specific leaching/runoff sheets, so this imports Input - Pasture plus 6.2 Pasture residue N2O and 6.3 Leach runoff pasture (which depends only on the pasture residue sheet and Input - Pasture, not on any crop sheet or Input - Crop). The crop input, 6.1 Crop residue N2O and 6.4 Residue burning are not required. A bare string (like the entries above) imports every sheet the registry lists for that module; this object form subsets them.
